title: The Indwelling of the Holy Spirit
intro: You are a temple. Not metaphorically, not aspirationally — actually, presently, permanently. The Holy Spirit does not visit you. He does not come and go with your spiritual seasons. He dwells within every believer from the moment of salvation, and He does not leave.
reading:
Romans 8:9 makes the indwelling the very mark of belonging to Christ: anyone who does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to Him. To be in Christ is to have the Spirit. There is no Christianity without the indwelling Holy Spirit.

1 Corinthians 6:19-20 presses the weight of this truth with practical force: your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, whom you have from God. You are not your own — you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body. The indwelling is not just a comfort. It is a claim. Your body belongs to God because God's Spirit inhabits it.

Ephesians 1:13-14 adds the language of sealing and guarantee: you were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit, who is the guarantee of our inheritance until we acquire possession of it. He is not a deposit that might be recalled. He is a seal — a permanent mark of ownership and security.

John 14:16-17 records Jesus' own promise: He will dwell with you and will be in you. Not beside you from a distance. In you. The God of the universe has taken up permanent residence within your spirit.

You are never alone. You carry the presence of God wherever you go.
reflection: How does the reality that the Holy Spirit permanently indwells you change the way you think about where you go, what you say, and how you treat your own body?
abide: Holy Spirit, I do not have to seek Your presence — You are already here. You dwell within me. Let me live with an awareness of that today — carrying You with me, yielding to You in every room, every conversation, every quiet moment.
